paper_title: Evaluating Alternative Metacommunity Hypotheses for Diatoms in the McMurdo Dry Valleys Using Simulations and Remote Sensing Data

methods.description: The MCSim metacommunity model is a spatially explicit, zero-sum lottery model based on Hubbell's neutral model, modified to include a dispersal kernel and an environmental filter. It simulates metacommunity dynamics over generations to study diatom community composition.

steps.description: Define the dispersal kernel W(r) as the probability of a successful dispersal event, where r is the distance between source and sink patches, and w is the slope of the dispersal kernel.
steps.searchable_summary: define dispersal kernel function for metacommunity model

steps.description: Calculate the recruitment probabilities using the environmental filter λ(E), which re-weights taxon abundance based on local environmental state E, taxon's optimal value µ, niche-breadth σ, and constant C niche.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate recruitment probabilities with environmental filter

steps.description: Simulate metacommunity dynamics over generations using initial taxon abundances and patch attributes, allowing J M lottery recruitment events per time step.

methods.method_name: Incorporating Environmental Filtering and Dispersal Traits
methods.description: This method involves refining the modeling approach to test predictions about community composition by incorporating environmental filtering using chlorophyll-a concentrations and considering diatom functional traits for dispersal abilities.

steps.description: Implement environmental filtering in MCSim using chlorophyll-a concentrations as a proxy for local stream harshness.
steps.searchable_summary: use chlorophyll-a as proxy for environmental filtering

steps.description: Use empirically observed diatom occurrence data in the OMI analysis to determine habitat preferences for each taxon in the environmental filtering process.
steps.searchable_summary: determine habitat preferences using OMI analysis

steps.description: Incorporate diatom functional traits to provide variation in dispersal abilities in the next generation of metacommunity models.
steps.searchable_summary: incorporate functional traits for dispersal variation
